response.justification: The candidate is a broadly experienced, craftsmanship-oriented full-stack engineer, but his profile does not match this role's core requirements. His professional C++ experience is limited to roughly seven months at National Instruments (2014–2015) plus a thesis project, far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. He lists general SQL/MySQL rather than SQL Server or Oracle, and he has no fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets experience — his closest domain exposure is a German fintech (RIDE Capital) built on Node/TypeScript. Strong engineering fundamentals do not offset the absence of both mandatory pillars.
strengths: Solid low-latency systems background (high-throughput DLang microservices with async I/O and caching)
Strong software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based development
Broad polyglot experience and proven ability to learn new stacks quickly
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, none of it Windows-platform focused
No fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain experience
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database work centers on MongoDB and MySQL

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ experience vs. 5–10 year mandate
No capital-markets or fixed-income domain knowledge
Strong general engineering and low-latency systems fundamentals
